items.title: How a grassroots revolution is safeguarding groundwater, one well at a time
items.description: A profile of Professor Basant Maheshwari and MARVI’s journey from rural India to a wider participatory model.

items.title: People power delivers groundwater solutions in India
items.description: How Bhujal Jankaars, recharge, MyWell and community decisions became a practical approach.

items.title: Satellite and on-the-ground data help monitor groundwater in India
items.description: Village observations and satellite information combine to support more efficient water use.

items.title: Securing groundwater through village communities
items.description: An introduction to participatory monitoring, recharge, cooperatives and the MARVI approach.

items.title: Bridging the gender gap through groundwater monitoring
items.description: Women Bhujal Jankaars and the social barriers shaping participation in local water decisions.

items.title: People managing the invisible
items.description: Farmers use measurements, recharge pits and MyWell to protect wells and inform local choices.

items.title: Revolutionary app reveals the resource underground
items.description: MyWell helps communities record and view groundwater, rainfall, quality and check-dam levels.

items.title: App helps Indian farmers crowdsource water during drought
items.description: Locally generated data helps communities monitor wells used for irrigation and drinking water.

items.title: Water management from the ground up
items.description: The central role of trained village volunteers in measuring and communicating groundwater.

items.title: India–Australia cooperation for sustainable groundwater
items.description: A memorandum supporting groundwater training, education, research and practical action.

items.title: A participatory framework for Southeast Asia
items.description: How MARVI’s community monitoring and collaborative data model may transfer across the region.

items.title: Developing a village groundwater-monitoring programme
items.description: The MARVI approach, Bhujal Jankaars and resources in English, Hindi and Gujarati.
